questions.question: The ancient Egyptians used $\sqrt{2}$ to represent the diagonal of a square. Rationalize the denominator of $\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}$.
The ancient Babylonians used $\sqrt{3}$ to represent the diagonal of a rectangle. Rationalize the denominator of $\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}}$.
The ancient Greeks used $\sqrt{5}$ to represent the golden ratio. Rationalize the denominator of $\frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}$.
The Mayans used $\sqrt{6}$ to represent the length of a bridge. Rationalize the denominator of $\frac{3}{\sqrt{6}}$.
Archimedes used $\sqrt{7}$ to calculate the area of a circle. Rationalize the denominator of $\frac{2}{\sqrt{7}}$.
Euclid used $\sqrt{8}$ to represent the length of a rectangle. Rationalize the denominator of $\frac{1}{\sqrt{8}}$.
The ancient Chinese used $\sqrt{9}$ to represent the area of a square. Rationalize the denominator of $\frac{3}{\sqrt{9}}$.
The ancient Indians used $\sqrt{10}$ to represent the length of a bridge. Rationalize the denominator of $\frac{2}{\sqrt{10}}$.
Rationalize the denominator of $\frac{5}{\sqrt{12}}$ and simplify. Show your work.
Rationalize the denominator of $\frac{4}{\sqrt{15}}$ and simplify. Show your work.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Remind students to simplify after rationalizing the denominator.
Tip 2: Encourage students to check their work.
Tip 3: Provide extra support for students who struggle with radicals.
